CLOSE BRIEFINGClose Briefing
Close briefing generates at 4:05 PM ET.
Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:02 ET
Dispersion bet emerges as index puts clash with single-name call surge
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedge — index puts 2.04x vs single-name calls 0.77x with implied correlation at just 12%.
  • 2. Energy sector blocks dominate with 90k XLE puts purchased ahead of CPI, contrasting tech strength in MRVL/AMD flows.
  • 3. Positive gamma environment ($10.4B SPY) should dampen moves, but CPI in 24 hours creates binary risk for vol expansion.
NARRATIVE — bullish flow bearish macro

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between indices and single names — index puts outnumber calls 2:1 while single-name calls dominate at 0.77x, the widest split in months. This classic dispersion setup aligns with implied correlation collapsing to 12%, creating the textbook environment where institutions hedge broad exposure while betting on stock-specific outperformance. The 79-point divergence between bullish flow (+70) and bearish regime (-9) confirms this positioning: smart money expects individual winners despite macro uncertainty. Energy hedging dominates block activity with 90,000 XLE puts purchased across multiple strikes, likely positioning for CPI volatility given oil's inflation sensitivity. The XLE vol surface shows the "VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT" divergence flag, suggesting these puts are defensive rather than speculative. Contrasting this defensive energy stance, semiconductor flows remain constructive with MRVL and AMD showing both technical strength (golden crosses, RSI >60) and continued institutional backing from Millennium's Q4 builds. The $10.4B positive gamma environment should theoretically dampen volatility, but CPI arrives in 24 hours with VIX term structure in steep contango (+1.47 pts). This setup creates asymmetric risk: if CPI surprises, the dispersion positioning could amplify individual stock moves while index hedges limit broad market damage.

Layer 1: Sentiment +38Layer 2: GEX +$10.4BLayer 3: P/C divergence 2.04/0.77Layer 4: Implied corr 12%Layer 9: CPI tomorrow

Massive 90,000 put block across multiple strikes ahead of CPI print. XLE showing VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT divergence flag, confirming defensive positioning rather than speculative bet. Energy's inflation sensitivity makes this a macro hedge.

VIAVVOL PLAY
Binary event volatility

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
